statement: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget.
The system shall allow only the IFA to control and monitor the budget balance.
The system shall allow specification of budget policies and generate alerts for policy violations.
The system shall allow the financial team to access the financial data of the teams.
The system shall allow administrative users to view diverse transactions.
The system shall allow referees to insert game events in real time using a mobile phone.
The system shall allow all users, including unregistered guests, to view game result data.
The system shall allow exporting data to journalists or press.
The system shall allow team managers and team administration to view data and perform offline analysis.
The system shall allow only teams to insert budget transactions.
The system shall restrict each team to access only its own data.
The system shall allow the IFA to view all monetary information, receive alerts, and request details, but shall prevent the IFA from modifying any data.
The system shall provide the ability to define scheduling policies.
The system shall provide guidance to allocate referees to games and shall avoid assigning the same referee to two games involving the same teams.
The system shall facilitate definition of policy and assign referees based on policy and referee preferences and availability.
The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
The system shall support schedule overrides by requiring a rationale explanation and confirmation from another IFA representative before applying the change.
The system shall enforce minimum gap days between matches as defined in the policy.
The system shall allow fans to subscribe to games or players and receive push notifications when events occur.
The system shall deliver notifications within the application/website and via mobile apps for referees and fans, and shall not send email notifications.
The system shall provide a unified backend with a web interface for administrators and teams and a mobile app interface for referees and fans.
The system shall restrict game data updates to official referees only, ensuring the system is the sole source of official game data.
The system shall send automatic reminder notifications, such as five minutes before a game starts.
The system shall support up to 50,000 concurrent users.
The system shall provide response times of one to two seconds for fan and admin interactions and deliver real-time high‑priority responses to referees.
The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent leakage.
The system shall support a team registration workflow in which a team manager submits documents, an IFA approves them, and thereafter the team can manage its resources.
The system shall retain all past scheduling archives.
The system shall allow the IFA to define leagues, define seasons, appoint and register referees, and set budget and scheduling policies.
